content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120105004136 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|lt=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|WD=Q135185635|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅中4丁目1番8号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:屯倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11465762}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120105004136 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|lt=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|WD=Q135185635|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅中4丁目1番8号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:屯倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11465762}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120105004136 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|lt=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|WD=Q135185635|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅中4丁目1番8号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:屯倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11465762}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120105004136 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|lt=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|WD=Q135185635|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅中4丁目1番8号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:屯倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11465762}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120105004136 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|lt=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|WD=Q135185635|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅中4丁目1番8号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:屯倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11465762}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:松原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Matsubara City]][[Category:qq]]

content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Matsubara City]]

content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|WD=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|WD=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|WD=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|WD=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|WD=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|WD=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|WD=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|WD=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Matsubara City]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120105004136 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|lt=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site|qid=Q135185635|1=Miyake Shrine (Matsubara) former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅中4丁目1番8号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府松原市三宅町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:屯倉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11465762}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Miyake Shrine |画像= [[画像:Miyake shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|qid=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}Miyakenaka 4-1-8 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 5（{{ill|lt=942|Q23740|qid=Q23740|1=942}}） |別名= Miyake Shrine, Miyake Tenmangū（old name） |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|qid=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Miyake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miyake Shrine'''（みやけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyakenaka, {{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|qid=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Shibagaki Shrine|Q11534936|qid=Q11534936|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Nunose Shrine|Q11480799|qid=Q11480799|1=Nunose Shrine}}, Gadō Hachimangū, {{ill|lt=Abo Shrine|Q122900553|qid=Q122900553|1=Ao Shrine (Matsubara)}} (all in Matsubara), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amamikoso Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka), it forms the "Matsubara Six Shrines Fortune Pilgrimage"[https://matsubara-kanko.net/enjoy/rokusya/ Matsubara Six Shrines Pilgrimage] Matsubara Tourism Association. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === History === It is said that in ancient times there was a Hohi shrine here enshrining {{ill|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|qid=Q10940685|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|qid=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}} (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|qid=Q1771125|1=Sugawara clan}}). According to the shrine document ''Miyake Tenmangū Baishōin Engi'', when Sugawara no Michizane was exiled to Dazaifu and passed through this land of his ancestors, he prayed at Hohi shrine and appealed his innocence[https://www.city.matsubara.osaka.jp/index.cfm/10,98,51,261,html Miyake Shrine and Tenjin worship - Matsubara City] viewed 2018-06-18. However, there are similar Michizane legends at places farther away in Osaka as well, and the full historical truth is unclear. For direct history connected to the present shrine, it is said that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|qid=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, a monk named Dōken received an oracle of Eleven-faced Kannon and cooperated with locals to enshrine Michizane in 942 at Ira-Miyake-gō, Tanboku District, Kawachi Province[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI/jinja-1/newpage1207.htm Miyake Shrine - Rediscover Osaka!] viewed 2018-06-18. The site is also said to have been associated with the residence of the {{ill|lt=Miyake clan|Q2975320|qid=Q2975320|1=Miyake clan}}, a local warrior group of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|qid=Q662881|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. In modern times, from around the Meiji period, the name shifted from Miyake Tenmangū to Miyake Shrine. === Cultural properties === A near life-size statue of Sugawara no Michizane is installed in the honden as a sacred image{{refnest|group=注釈|overall height 99.7 cm, overall width 128.3 cm, knee span 78 cm.}}. Tradition says Michizane reflected himself in a mirror at Dazaifu, carved this image, and sent it to this place; generally, however, the torso is considered an early-modern work. Other Tenjin-related cultural materials remain, including Konoe Nobuhiro's self-painted "Tōto Tenjin image," an image titled "Sugawara no Michizane portrait" said to be in the hand of Emperor Go-Yōzei, and Konoe Motohiro's calligraphic title "Namu Tenman Daijizaitenjin." === Precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake-dera|1=UNKNOWN}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. Now abolished. === Subsidiary shrines === There are subsidiary shrines including {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine (Matsubara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Shrine}}, Hiyoshi Shrine, Ōmononushi Shrine, Susanoo Shrine, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Ichikishima Shrine. === Sakaya Shrine === As a precinct shrine there is '''Sakaya Shrine'''. The deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhayahino-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and originally stood at today's Matsubara-shi Miyakenaka 6-2-2, but in 1907 it was moved into Miyake Shrine precincts[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112937/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/041307-01.html Sakaya Shrine] viewed 2018-06-18. === Local information === ;Address *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsubara|Q774952|qid=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyakenaka|1=UNKNOWN}}4-1-8 ;Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kawachi-Matsubara Station|Q6379458|qid=Q6379458|1=Kawachi-Matsubara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|Q901342|qid=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, about 21 minutes on foot (1.7 km north). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q11465761|qid=Q11465761|1=Miyake}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|qid=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Miyake-jinja (Matsubara)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai6shibu/matsubara-city/06021miyakejinja.html Miyake Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office] {{ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Matsubara)]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:松原市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Matsubara City]]
